observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to (i) (i) conduct a comprehensive physical count of all properties annually; (ii) reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records; (iii) prepare and submit the corresponding inventory reports in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; (iv) report all the unserviceable properties in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) supported with proper documentation for subsequent disposal of same in accordance with COA rules and regulations; (v) avail the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability; and (vi) establish the unit cost/value of PPE items found at station and facilitate the reconciliation of RPCPPE with property records and accounting records in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
b. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each item of PPE and periodically reconcile the two records.
observations.managementAction: (i) Implemented (i)The inventory committee conducted a physical count of all properties. (ii) Implemented (ii) and (iii), the Inventory committee conducted physical inventory and conducted a reconciled inventory report. (iii) Implemented (iii) The Inventory Committee prepared and submitted a reconciled inventory report. (iv) Implemented (iv)Prepared the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) supported with proper documentation for the disposal. (v) Not Implemented (v)The lack of knowledgeable staff that will handle the One Time Cleansing of PPE. (vi) Implemented (vi)The Municipal Accountant traced the unit cost/value of PPEs found in station from the procurement documents.
observations.reason: For (v) and a part of (b): Lack of knowledgeable staff that will handle the One Time Cleansing of PPE.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. set-up at the beginning of the year the amounts due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Register/Index Card to be provided by the Municipal Treasurer; b. trace the discrepancies noted in the RPT/SET Receivables and their corresponding Deferred Income accounts and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct their balances; c. refrain from derecognizing uncollected RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year to ensure proper recording and monitoring of delinquencies; d. use the appropriate accounts in the recording of collections of prior year/’s taxes, discounts on prompt payments and penalties; and e. initially record the discounts rewarded for the advance payments made by the taxpayers as debit to Discount on Advance Payments account. Thereafter, debit the Discount on RPT - Basic/SET account and credit the Discount on Advance Payments account for the application of discount.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The RPT/SET receivables set-up for CY 2024 were supported with the Real Property Tax Account provided by the Municipal Treasurer. Implemented The discrepancies between the abstract and RTP/SET Receivables had already been adjusted in January 2024. Implemented The Municipal Treasurer prepared an updated list of RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2024, and an adjusting entry was made to recognize the uncollected real property taxes of prior years. Implemented Collections of prior years’ taxes, discounts on prompt payments and penalties were recorded using Prior Period Adjustments, Discount on RPT-Basic/SET, and Tax Revenue-Fines and Penalties-Property Taxes account, respectively. Not Implemented The Accounting Office erroneously used outdated chart of accounts.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office erroneously used outdated chart of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. prepare the necessary journal entries for the erroneous recording of the transactions discussed above; b. cause the immediate refund of the unexpended balances totaling P10,248.81 to the Department of Agriculture and/or National Treasury pursuant to Item 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Item 5.0 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2021-13 and prepare the necessary liquidation report to reflect the return of the unexpended balances; c. coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to determine the amount to be remitted to the Bureau of Treasury, pursuant to DILG-DPWH JMC No. 001; and d. coordinate with the concerned implementing offices and/or the source agencies on the pertinent information of the non-moving balances for a clear understanding of the transactions/purposes of the funds received. Thereafter, take the necessary actions to achieve fair presentation of the accounts affected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implemented Adjusting entries were made to correct the subject transactions. Not Implemented The refund could not be facilitated due to the absence of supporting documents. Implemented The five percent share of the collection of building permit fees and other fees were remitted to the Bureau of Treasury. Not Implemented The Municipal Accountant already coordinated with the Department of Health and DBM relative to the unliquidated grants received by the Municipality. However, the office of the Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling/retrieving the documents pertaining to this account.
observations.reason: The refund could not be facilitated due to the absence of supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling/retrieving the documents pertaining to this account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to:
a. for projects with noted cost deficiencies, (i) investigate the cause/s of the defects/deficiencies, identify the parties to be held responsible for the repainting/ retouching, removal, and replacement thereof, as the case may be, and submit to the Audit Team sufficient pictures of the rectification of the defective works in accordance with Section 62.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (ii) closely monitor the progress of construction works and maintain the project logbook to record the daily project activities to validate the contractor’s compliance with the project specifications and to protect the interest of the government; b. for Project No. 1, submit the revised Construction Schedule Bar Chart and S-Curve, if any, for evaluation/validation by the TAG; c. closely monitor defects noted for Project Nos. 9 and 11 to prevent any serious impact on the structure; d. for Project Nos. 1 and 3, submit a satisfactory explanation on the contract cost which was found higher than the COA allowable estimate, for evaluation/validation by the TAG; and e. properly prepare detailed engineering prior to the implementation of the project to avoid overpricing of materials and overestimating quantities of materials and works.
observations.managementAction: (i) Not Implemented For Items a(i) and a(ii) the Audit Team requested for updates on rectifications made by the contractor on the concerned projects in order to request for their re-inspection. However, as of date, none was received by the Audit Team. (ii) Implemented The Municipal Engineer submitted the revised Construction Schedule Bar Chart and S-Curve to COA TAG for further evaluation. The COA TAG Report is yet to be received by the Audit Team. (iii) Implemented The Municipal Engineer conducted inspection on the subject isolation facilities. (iv) Implemented For Project No. 1, the Municipal Engineer submitted a written justification and this was submitted to COA TAG for re-evaluation. Also, the COA TAG noted that the submitted backup computation for the overestimated quantity of Item No. 506 (1) - Stone Masonry under Project No. 3 was found to be in order. (v) Implemented For CY 2024 infrastructure projects, the Approved Budget for the Contract is prepared in accordance with DPWH Department Order No. 197 series of 2016 dated October 7, 2016.
observations.reason: For Items a(i) and a(ii): the Audit Team requested updates on rectifications but, as of date, none was received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC, its Secretariat and TWG to:
a. refrain from indicating the units specific unit model of the equipment to be procured and/or “tailor fitting” its description to a specific brand unless it is necessary to maintain the performance, functionality, and useful life of existing equipment; b. promptly invite observers in all the stages of procurement pursuant to the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; c. ensure compliance with the prescribed period in the issuance of supplemental/bid bulletin; d. scrutinize the documents submitted by the bidder and exercise diligence in the evaluation of bids, particularly the post-qualification process during which the related responsiveness of the bids is being determined and properly disqualify the bidder if the documents are found to be lacking or deficient; e. notify the losing bidder of the recommendation of awarding the contract within three calendar days from the issuance of the resolution; f. ensure that all the necessary documents are complete before contract signing; and g. comply with the posting requirements provided in Section 37.1.6 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to ensure transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The BAC ensures that all documents forming part of the contract are properly attached thereto. Implemented The implementing units refrained from indicating the specific model of the equipment to be procured. Implemented Invitation to the observers were sent within the period prescribed under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Not Implemented Compliance with the prescribed period in the issuance of supplemental/bid when procurement activities are postponed due to lack of knowledge on the proper procedures to be undertaken. Not Implemented BAC reviewed documents submitted by the bidder, however various designations and simultaneous tasks of the BAC-TWG affected the quality of oversight in the performance of their procurement-related duties. Implemented The losing bidders were duly notified of the recommendation. Implemented The BAC complied with posting requirements of Notice of Award, Contract and Notice of Award.
observations.reason: Not Implemented items due to lack of knowledge on procedures and overlapping designations and simultaneous tasks of BAC-TWG affecting oversight.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC and the Municipal Engineer to carefully plan the projects to be charged against the 20 percent DF and regularly monitor its implementation to deliver timely services to the intended beneficiaries thereof.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Ineffective planning, lack of monitoring mechanisms, and the absence of a systematic review and reallocation process by the MDC.
observations.reason: Ineffective planning, lack of monitoring mechanisms, and the absence of a systematic review and reallocation process by the MDC.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. MDRRMO to prepare and submit a comprehensive LDRRMFIP by (i) incorporating the PPAs charged against the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of previous years and continuing appropriations in the LDRRMFIP and integrating those PPAs in the AIP of the Municipality; (ii) refraining from allocating LDRRMF for Personal Services of the MDRRM Office; (iii) preparing a revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP and AIP reflecting those PPAs that were not initially included in the original LDRRMFIP and AIP to support the disbursements of the fund; and (iv) ensuring the viability of PPAs charged against the fund to attain optimum utilization thereof; b. Municipal Engineer to ensure that PPAs implemented by administration are supported with the documentary requirements enumerated under Item 9.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, as the case may be; and c. Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure compliance with the recording and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; (ii) ensure the accuracy of the amount presented in the RUDDRMF and the Notes to Financial Statements; and (iii) see to it that disbursements are proper and supported with complete and appropriate documents.
observations.managementAction: Implemented (a)(i) PPAs charged against the unexpended balance of previous years and continuing appropriations were incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and AIP of the Municipality. (a)(ii) Implemented However, PPAs charged against the continuing appropriations were not included in the LDRRMFIP. (a)(iii) Implemented Prepared a Supplemental LDRRMFIP to include new expenditure charged against the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF. (a)(iv) Implemented PPAs charged against the fund were implemented during the year. Not Implemented (b) The recording of the payment of wages of laborers was mistakenly recorded under Due to NGAs account instead of Trust Liabilities – DRRMF, thus understating the unexpended balance of the DRRM-MOOE that is transferred to the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF. Implemented (c)(i) Ensured compliance with recording and reporting guidelines under applicable COA circulars. Not Implemented (c)(ii) Coordinated with the MDRRMO in the preparation of RUDDRMF but overlooked the payment of wages of laborers. Not Implemented (c)(iii) The notes to financial statements for the receipt of donated goods was not yet disclosed; the Municipal Accountant will implement this in preparation of NFS for FY 2025.
observations.reason: For some items: PPAs charged against the continuing appropriations were not included in the LDRRMFIP; payment of wages of laborers mistakenly recorded under Due to NGAs; notes disclosure for donated goods pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. members of the GAD Focal Point System to: (i) ensure compliance with the budgetary requirements for GAD; (ii) be guided by the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 on the proper planning and budgeting for GAD Fund especially on the attribution of LGU programs and projects to GAD budget; and (iii) see to it that the GPB and GAD AR are supported with complete documents.
b. implementing units to consider the proper procurement procedures in the implementation of GAD-related PPAs and adhere with the rules and regulations prescribed by the procurement law; c. Municipal Engineer to prepare and submit all required documents previously mentioned to adequately support the implementation of the PPAs by administration; and d. Municipal Accountant to ensure that the supporting documents are complete before processing the payments.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipality complied with the budgetary requirements for GAD. Not Implemented Lack of knowledge on how to properly present the attributed PPAs in the GPB. Implemented The GAD AR was supported with a summary of the reported program or project and results of Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) tests. Implemented The Municipal Engineer ensured development projects were implemented according to the purpose for which they were created. Implemented The Municipal Accountant secures all required documents when processing claims. The GAD Focal Person and Municipal Budget Officer to be guided by provisions and prepare a revised GPB when necessary. GAD Focal Person to ensure documentation; Municipal Accountant to check completeness.
observations.reason: Lack of knowledge on how to properly present the attributed PPAs in the GPB; some supporting documents still being compiled for submission to DILG and COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as the Chairperson of the LSB direct its members and Secretariat to: a. coordinate with the Local Finance Committee and other concerned stakeholders, to ensure strict compliance with the prescribed budget calendar set out in Section 5.5 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, by implementing measures that promote timely budget planning and strengthen inter-agency coordination for the prompt preparation, approval, and implementation of SEF-funded PPAs; b. facilitate the timely submission of the SEF budget to the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer since the SEF budget serves as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes; and c. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as the preparer of the SEF utilization report to ensure the regular submission of quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form 1 on SEF utilization, to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of DBM and DILG pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, S.2017.
observations.managementAction: Implemented The Municipal Accountant prepared the quarterly SEF utilization reports and consistently submitted them to the DILG in compliance with the requirement to post the Report of SEF Utilization in the Full Disclosure Policy Portal (FDPP). The SEF utilization reports for CY 2024 were posted. The Municipal Treasurer, Budget Officer were not furnished by the LSB of the approved SEF budget within the reglementary period after the approval. The LSB failed to transmit the SEF quarterly and annual utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office through the appropriate channels and to furnish copies to the Regional Office of the DBM. Municipal Accountant has raised the issue during LSB meeting and concerned parties committed to comply.
observations.reason: LSB failed to transmit SEF quarterly and annual utilization reports to DepEd Central Office and to furnish copies to DBM and DILG; delays in furnishing approved SEF budget to relevant municipal officers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned officials and department heads to review the audit findings relevant to their respective functions and submit the corresponding actions taken or planned for inclusion in the AAPSI. If necessary, a focal person may be designated to coordinate with the responsible officials and consolidate the required information. This will help promote accountability, facilitate the effective monitoring of corrective actions, and support the timely resolution of audit issues.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The agency representatives agreed with the recommendations and promised compliance thereto.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Agriculturist furnish to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned above.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented Since the Municipal Agriculturist was not present during the exit conference, the concerns will just be relayed to her by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the ICO - Municipal Assessor to furnish to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned above.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The Municipal Engineer explained that the Municipality is awaiting the outcome of negotiations for the procurement of the adjacent lot, which is also owned by the same seller. This is to facilitate a smoother and more efficient processing of the property’s registration.
observations.reason: Awaiting outcome of negotiations for procurement of adjacent lot to facilitate smoother processing of property's registration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise all appropriate legal remedies to enforce the settlement of the remaining balance of the audit disallowance. This includes initiating necessary collection actions against other liable parties to ensure full recovery of public funds and uphold the principles of accountability and fiscal responsibility. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit subsidiary ledgers and schedule of payments of persons liable on the disallowances issued prior to CY 2022 to reconcile with the balance of Receivable-Disallowances/ Charges Account.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented The agency representatives assured the Audit Team of their commitment to comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each item of PPE and
periodically reconcile these records to ensure accuracy of the reported inventory.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor reconstitute the
inventory committee to ensure proper checks and balances, and provide adequate
training and orientation on inventory procedures and internal control standards
to committee members to ensure that a competent and capable team handles this
critical function.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. correct the accounting records to reflect the actual closure of bank
accounts, and ensure that all reconciling items are valid, clearly identified,
and properly adjusted in the books;

b. ensure that the specific source and destination bank accounts are clearly
identified in the journal entries, and that each entry includes a detailed
explanation of the transaction when recording inter-bank fund transfers; and

c. strengthen internal controls over cash management and journal entry
preparation to ensure the accuracy, traceability, and reliability of financial
data.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to initially record the
discounts rewarded for the advance payments made by the taxpayers as debit to
Discount on Advance Payments account. Thereafter, debit the Discount on RPT
- Basic/SET account and credit the Discount on Advance Payments account for
the application of discount;

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor and other concerned
officials to:

a. undertake a comprehensive review of all delinquent accounts, particularly
those outstanding for more than five years, to determine their
collectability, if warranted, recognize Allowance for Impairment –
RPT/SET; and

b. regularly update the List of Delinquent Taxpayers to avoid further
accumulation of aged receivables.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. cause the immediate refund of the unexpended balances totaling
P10,248.81 to the DA and/or National Treasury pursuant to Item 4.9
of COA Circular No. 94-013 and Item 5.0 of DBM CL No. 2021-13 and
prepare the necessary liquidation report to reflect the return of the
unexpended balances; and

b. coordinate with the concerned implementing offices and/or the source
agencies to obtain pertinent information on the non-moving balances for a
clear understanding of the transactions/purposes of the funds received. In
the event that these dormant fund transfers can no longer be traced due to
absence of documents, take the necessary actions to avail of the one-time
cleansing of dormant accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2023-008 to
achieve fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with GSIS, Pag-
IBIG and PhilHealth and make the necessary adjustments; and

b. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to always exert effort in exploring
alternative solutions to promptly address any future issues in the
remittance of mandatory contributions, to prevent delays and avoid
penalties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. require the concerned Requisitioning Units to use the DPRI as guide in the
determination of the ABC in the procurement of drugs and medicines to
comply with the DOH A.O. No. 2019-0040; and

b. direct the Requisitioning Units to (i) submit written
explanation/justification for their non-compliance with the requirements
provided under E.O. No. 49, s. 1993 and the inconsistencies noted in the
preparation of the PR; and (ii) henceforth, request only drugs and
medicines listed in the PNF. If procurement of medicines not in the list is
necessary, apply for inclusion of medicines in the PNF. Please note that
applications for inclusion are only accepted within the month of January
of each year. The proponent shall completely accomplish the PNF form
for inclusion, which shall be signed by the Pharmaceuticals and
Therapeutic Committee (PTC) Chair for hospitals. The form maybe
downloaded from the Pharmaceutical Division website,
www.pharmadiv.doh.gov.ph. Application may also be done online
through: http://www.pnf.doh.gov.ph;
To procure non-PNF medicines that are urgently needed, apply for
exemption from E.O. No. 49 s. 1993. The health facility shall completely
accomplish the PNF form for exemption, which shall be signed by the PTC
Chair for hospitals. The form maybe downloaded from the Pharmaceutical
Division website, www.pharmadiv.doh.gov.ph;

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC, its
Secretariat, and its TWG to:

a. consolidate the PPMPs of the various implementing units into a
comprehensive APP to facilitate efficient and effective procurement
activities;

b. ensure that the stipulations in the bidding documents are consistent with
the mode of procurement being applied, to avoid confusion and to uphold
the integrity and legality of the procurement process;

c. diligently evaluate the Statement of SLCC and ensure strict compliance
with the requirements stipulated in the Bidding Documents. Any deviation
from these requirements should warrant the disqualification of non-
compliant bidders, in accordance with the guidelines set forth by the
Revised IRR;

d. ensure strict adherence to the posting requirements for all Notices issued
by the BAC, and take appropriate measures to guarantee that all
stakeholders are properly informed, in line with the principles of
transparency and accountability in public procurement; and

e. attend training to strengthen the understanding and compliance with the
procurement laws and regulations.

For procurements made after the effectivity date (February 25, 2025) of the IRR
of R.A. No. 12009, or the New Government Procurement Act (NGPA), the
management is reminded to observe strictly its provisions and its IRR in the
procurement of goods and infrastructure projects to ensure adherence to the
principles of transparency, competitiveness, efficiency, proportionality,
accountability, public monitoring, procurement professionalization, and
sustainability in government procurement.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct
the BAC and its TWG to:

a. scrutinize the documents submitted by the bidder and exercise diligence in
the evaluation of bids, particularly the post-qualification process during
which the responsiveness of the bids is being determined, and properly
disqualify the bidder in the event that the documents are found to be
lacking or deficient;

b. review and/or revise internal control policies to address recurring issues
relative to the preparation and handling of procurement documents;

c. establish valid and practical communication protocols and ensure that
these are clearly stipulated in the bidding documents so that all bidders are
aware of how official notices and other communications to and from the
BAC will be transmitted to ensure compliance with the timelines set by the
procurement law;

d. ensure that contracts are perfected within the period prescribed under
Section 37.2 of the Revised IRR to ensure efficiency and integrity of the
procurement process; and

e. attend training to strengthen the understanding and compliance with the
procurement laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as Chairperson of the MDC
Executive Committee, instruct the MPDC to:

a. strengthen its planning process by ensuring that all projects proposed for
funding under the 20% DF are thoroughly assessed for feasibility and
implementation readiness prior to their inclusion in the AIP to ensure
optimal fund utilization and timely delivery of benefits to its intended
beneficiaries;

b. coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and other concerned
departments to establish the fund monitoring and reporting system to
support planning, resource management, and evidence-based decision-
making; and

c. conduct an immediate review of all unexpended balances and savings
from prior years’ PPAs funded under the 20 percent DF, and formulate
and approve a reprogramming plan to realign the said funds to priority
and viable development projects, as identified in the current AIP.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor instruct the:

a. MDRRMO to (i) ensure consistent classification of allotment class of the
PPAs in the LDRRMFIP and AIP; (ii) closely monitor not only the status
of implementation of DRRM-related PPAs but also the utilization of the
LDRRMF; (iii) ensure that all PPAs to be charged against the LDRRMF
are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP and AIP and that expenditures
relative thereto are DRRM-related; (iv) submit the revised LDRRMFIP for
CY 2024, if there’s any, showing the allocation for gasoline for which the
2023 expenditures were charged; and (v) support the fuel consumption
with the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption, and
Monthly Report of Official Travels as prescribed by the Manual on Audit
of Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles;

b. MSWDO to (i) coordinate with the Accounting Office on the receipt of
donated goods for DRRM to ensure the proper recording thereof; and
(ii) prepare and maintain the Acknowledgment Receipt for In-Kind
Donations for DRRM and Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations -
DRRM, Annexes D and E, respectively, as required under COA Circular
No. 2024-002 to ensure transparency and accountability on donated
goods; and

c. Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct the balance of Trust Liabilities – DRRMF and Road Networks
account; (ii) ensure the accuracy of the amount presented in the
RUDDRMF and the Notes to Financial Statements; and (iv) disclose the
receipt of donated goods in the Notes to Financial Statements.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the Municipal
Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. GAD Focal person and Municipal Budget Officer to be guided by the
provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-001 on the
proper planning and budgeting for the GAD Fund, especially on the
attribution of LGU programs and projects to GAD budget;

b. Chairperson, GPFS-TWG/GAD Focal Person to prepare a revised GPB, if
necessary, to accommodate changes in the original GPB after the receipt
of the approved/enacted Annual and Supplemental Budgets and inform the
concerned DILG Office in writing of the changes made thereto pursuant to
Item 6 (C) (7.2) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01;

c. GAD Focal Person to ensure that the GPB and GAD AR are supported
with complete documents for proper evaluation and assessment; and

d. Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and accuracy of all
supporting documents before recording transactions in the books of
accounts, to uphold the reliability and integrity of the financial records and
to ensure proper utilization of funds.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal
Mayor, as the Chairperson of the LSB direct its members and Secretariat to:

a. coordinate with the Local Finance Committee and other concerned
stakeholders, to ensure strict compliance with the prescribed budget
calendar set out in Section 5.5 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1,
S. 2017, by implementing measures that promote timely budget planning
and strengthen inter-agency coordination for the prompt preparation,
approval, and implementation of SEF-funded PPAs;

b. facilitate the timely submission of the SEF budget to the Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer since the SEF
budget serves as basis for disbursement or certification of availability of
funds and for recording purposes; and

c. coordinate with the Municipal Accountant as the preparer of the SEF
utilization report to ensure the regular submission of quarterly and annual
reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form 1 on SEF utilization, to
the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, copy furnished
the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of DBM and DILG
pursuant to Item 6.1 of the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, S.2
017.

Given the above observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor,
thru the HRMO and in coordination with all the departments, create a committee
that would formulate the required staffing plan and standard policy guidelines or
procedure on the hiring of personnel under Job-orders, Contractual Personnel
and Consultants, to ascertain the propriety, necessity and regularity thereof.

We further recommended that the HRMO strictly comply with the
provisions of COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, Series of 2020, and COA Circular
No. 2009-001, particularly regarding the timely submission of JO contracts and
supporting documents to the Audit Team for proper review and evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct all concerned
officials and department heads to review the audit findings relevant to their
respective functions and submit the corresponding actions taken or planned
for inclusion in the AAPSI. If necessary, a focal person may be designated to
coordinate with the responsible officials and consolidate the required
information. This will help promote accountability, facilitate the effective
monitoring of corrective actions, and support the timely resolution of audit
issues.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Agriculturist furnish to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned
above.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the above persons to refund the honoraria paid to them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the ICO -
Municipal Assessor to furnish to the Audit Team the lacking documents
mentioned above.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise all appropriate
legal remedies to enforce the settlement of the remaining balance of the audit
disallowance. This includes initiating necessary collection actions against other
liable parties to ensure full recovery of public funds and uphold the principles
of accountability and fiscal responsibility.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant
prepare and submit subsidiary ledgers and schedule of payments of persons
liable on the disallowances issued prior to CY 2022 to reconcile with the
balance of Receivable-Disallowances/ Charges Account.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to initially recognize the appropriate Inventory account for every purchase of inventories and derecognize the same upon issuance of the items to the end-user offices; and (SSMI) report submitted by the Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer.
b. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain and regularly update the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, for each type of inventory and periodically reconcile the two records.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed that:
the Inventory Committee (i) reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records to determine the correctness of property records and any possible losses occasioned by theft or other casualty; (ii) furnish the Audit Team with the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE duly reconciled with the property records and accounting records pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1; and  (iii) check the condition of the properties, report in the IIRUP and Waste Material Report, the damaged, obsolete and unserviceable properties identified in the course of inventory-taking and immediately request for their disposal;
the Municipal Accountant ensure that (i) necessary adjusting entries be made to reflect the correct balances of the Office Equipment and Information and Communication Technology Equipment Account; and
proper disclosure of PPEs be made in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 17.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, being the Chairperson of the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, spearhead the preparation of a comprehensive and reliable LDRRMFIP which (i) clearly identify the disaster preparedness, response and mitigation activities of the Municipality; (ii) classify the PPAs into their proper allotment class to avoid inconsistencies between the budget and the corresponding disbursements in accordance with the existing rules and regulations; (iii) incorporate those PPAs charged against the Trust Liability – DRRM account in the LDRRMFIP; and (iv) integrate those PPAs in the AIP of the Municipality.  We also recommended that a revised/supplemental LDRRMFIP be prepared for any changes made on the budget.
We further recommended that the MDRRMO effectively and efficiently implement PPAs funded out of the LDRRMF.
Lastly, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) record grants and donations to the Trust Liability – DRRM account; (ii) ensure compliance with the recording and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002, COA Circular No. 2014-002, and COA Circular 2016-004; and (iii) ensure that disbursements are proper and supported with complete and appropriate documents.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160.   The Municipal Mayor should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with.  As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow payments only of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan, in their review of the annual and supplemental budgets of the Municipality, ensure that the LGU’s budgets comply with statutory and administrative requirements.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to comply with the documentary requirements on payments as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184; and (b) ensure that the submitted surety bond by the contractors represents the liability it guarantees to protect the best interest of the Municipality.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) classify  and record the taxes collected to the appropriate accounts and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries on the erroneous recording of collections of taxes on the extraction of sand, gravel and other quarry products to fairly present the Financial Statements of the Municipality.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Mayor, as the HOPE, direct the BAC to: (i) promote and ensure transparency, competitiveness, accountability, equity, efficiency and economy in the procurement process; (ii) properly evaluate the mode of procurement to be used; (iii) ensure that the conditions for the use of alternative mode of procurement are met; and (iv) strictly adhere with the provisions set forth by the procurement law.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant require necessary supporting documents before making payments.
We further recommended that the concerned personnel furnish to the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the recorded financial liabilities are valid obligations of the Municipality and are duly supported with appropriate documents.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare necessary adjusting entries to properly reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF account;  (ii) disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements those information not evident in the face of the Financial Statements so that the users thereof would have better understanding on the Municipality’s financial status; (iii) ensure compliance with the recording and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular 2012-002, COA Circular No. 2014-002 and COA Circular 2020-009; and (iv) ensure that payments are supported with complete and appropriate documents;
direct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to effectively and efficiently implement PPAs funded out of the LDRRMF; and
direct the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that procurement of goods and services are done with eligible suppliers.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
Municipal Development Council to properly identify priority projects and prepare a comprehensive development plan in accordance with the guidelines set forth for the utilization of the 20% Development Fund;
Municipal Engineer to conduct detailed engineering activities for each development projects to ensure effective and efficient implementation thereof.  For projects to be implemented by phase, ensure that there is clear delineation of work for each phase; and
MPDC to monitor and evaluate the implementation of the different development programs, projects, and activities in the Municipality in accordance with the approved development plan as well as comply with the provisions of the JMC.
Municipal Accountant (i) to submit the lacking documents relative to the above disbursements to avoid possible suspension in audit; and (b) ensure the completeness and appropriateness of documents supporting payments to be made in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the end-users or implementing units to specify in the PPMPs the manner of implementation of the project whether by administration or straight contract.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to (a) properly prepare and use the 6th edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents in their future procurement activities; (b) scrutinize the documents submitted by the bidder during post-qualification process; (c) adhere with the period of action for each procurement process; (d) ensure the completeness of supporting documents before recommending the award of contract; and (e) adhere with the provisions set forth by the procurement law in their future procurements.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160.   The Municipal Mayor should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with.  As part of his fiscal responsibility, he should allow payments only of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.

We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer adhere to the provisions of LBC No. 103 in the computation of the RATA of municipal officials and employees.  Also, direct the concerned SB Members to refund the excess RATA that were paid to them.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan adopt a more specific Internal Rules of Procedures relative to the attendance to sessions and disciplinary measures on absences as provided in Section 50 (b) (5), Title II, Book I of the LGC.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that required supporting documents of the payrolls are complete pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Management Officer properly computed the RATA of the municipal officials and employees for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:

refund of honoraria paid to the MDRRMO-Designee;

Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and reconcile the balances of the accounts affected by the transfer and use of LDRRM Fund.  Moreover, disclose properly the receipt of donated assets in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-02; and

MDRRMO to ensure compliance with the minimum requirements of equipage and pre-positioned food packs and hygiene kits are in place so that the Municipality could timely and efficiently respond in times of calamities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Human Resource and Management Officer adhere to the provisions of LBC No. 103 in the computation of the RATA of municipal officials and employees.  Also, direct the concerned SB Members to refund the excess RATA that were paid to them.
We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan adopt a more specific Internal Rules of Procedures relative to the attendance to sessions and disciplinary measures on absences as provided in Section 50 (b) (5), Title II, Book I of the LGC.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that required supporting documents of the payrolls are complete pursuant to Section 4(6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor stop the payment of honoraria to officers and employees for the services performed which are deemed part of their functions, duties and responsibilities.  Also, direct the above persons to refund the honoraria that were paid to them.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant who is in charge of the accounting and internal audit services of the Municipality ensure the legality and propriety of the payments made.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the implementing departments to ensure that barangay officials first secure written permission from the DILG before the Municipality can engage their services.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant who is in charge of the accounting and internal audit services of Municipality ensure the legality and propriety of the payments made.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
refund of honoraria paid to the MDRRMO – Designee;
Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and reconcile the balances of the accounts affected by the transfer and use of LDRRM Fund.  Moreover, disclose properly the receipt of donated assets in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2014-02; and
MDRRMO to ensure compliance with the minimum requirements of equipage and pre-positioned food packs and hygiene kits are in place so that the Municipality could timely and efficiently respond in times of calamities.

Based on the foregoing, we recommended that the:
Municipal Development Council adhere to the guidelines on the project identification and inclusion in the AIP of development projects to be funded by the 20% DF; and
Municipal Engineer to conduct detailed engineering activities for each development projects to ensure effective and efficient implementation thereof.  For projects to be implemented by phase, ensure that there is clear delineation of work for each phase.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor reorganize and/or strengthen the LGU’s GAD Focal Point System and ensure that all members of the GFPS undergo capability building programs on GAD as stated in Section 4.1 (A) (3) of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-1.
We also recommended that the GAD Focal Person seek the assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the gathering of sex-disaggregated data and GAD-related information for the establishment and maintenance of GAD Data Base and the use of and/or administration of the HGDG.

We recommended that the MPDC plan and coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the implementation schedule of infrastructure projects especially those implemented by administration to properly facilitate the procurement activities relative thereto.
We also recommended the BAC and its Secretariat to (a) consolidate the PPMPs of various implementing units and prepare the APP; and (b) adopt the proper procurement in accordance with the procurement law.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the BAC:
furnish the Audit Team with copies of Purchase Orders and their supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection thereof; and
ensure compliance with the requirements prescribed under the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 to prevent the same lapses in future procurements.
Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the investigation on the procurement procedures adopted by the BAC on the subject ten projects and hold accountable those found remiss of their respective functions and duties.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the BAC furnish the Audit Team with copies of Contract Agreements and their supporting documents within five days after perfection thereof and ensure compliance with the requirements prescribed under the RIRR of RA No. 9184 to prevent the same lapses in future procurements.
Meanwhile, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the investigation on the procurement procedures adopted by the BAC on the subject five projects and hold accountable those found remiss of their respective functions and duties.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that:
the Local Finance Committee identify additional sources of income;
the Municipal Treasurer determine doable measures to improve collection efficiency of internally sourced taxes, fees and charges; and
the Local Chief Executive execute contract of lease with stallholder.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the:
Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the payment of the salaries or any amount due to the persons liable for the settlement of their liabilities;
the Municipal Mayor request for installment payment provided that a written authority is issued by the persons liable in case where there are several persons liable in accordance with COA Resolution No. 2017-021; and
Municipal Accountant submits subsidiary ledgers and schedule of payments of persons liable on the subject disallowances to reconcile with the balance of Receivable – Disallowances/Charges Account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure written approval from the Governor for any official travel outside the province in accordance with Section 96 of R.A. 7160.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant thoroughly check the supporting documents before making payments and properly record registration fees as part of Training Expenses account to properly reflect the correct amounts in the Financial Statements.

Ensure completeness of information on the face of RCD such as date and number of the report, the date of verification and acknowledgement and signature of the signatories. 

The Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the amounts of the recorded transactions and adjust the balance of the accounts affected by the miscalculated collections, as appropriate.
observations.reason: Delay in the remittance of collections were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP to the Audit Team in accordance with Item 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012




We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to adhere strictly with Item 5.1.10 COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 by transferring all unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the special trust fund and by preparing a detailed breakdown by year of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF of prior years to current year to reflect the correct balance of account Trust Liability- DRRM, Accounts 438 in the Trust Fund book.  
  
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adhere to the requirements of Section 2 of Rule 6 of the IRR of R.A. No. 10121 and further be guided by the provisions of NDRRMC/DILG/DBM/CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.
observations.reason: The Municipality failed to submit to the Audit Team its LDRRMFIP for CY 2016. Also, it failed to transfer the actual cash or recognize an obligation for the unexpended balance of the 30% RQF and DRRM-MOOE for CY 2016 to the Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management review and evaluate the terms and conditions of the loans and exert extra efforts in retrieving their co-makers so that collection may be effected from them.  Moreover, we recommend that Management remind the Accounting Department to provide complete subsidiary records of the names, addresses and individual balances of debtors in accordance with Section 64, Volume 1 of the GAAM.  Further, develop a more effective collection strategy on the collection of the loans. Otherwise, apply for write-off of the accounts through a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting authority to write-off from the Commission on Audit stating the reasons and extent of follow-up made.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts were still classified as current assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC to observe strictly the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 specifically in the procurement of infrastructure projects to ensure adherence to the principles of transparency, competitiveness and public monitoring and to properly address the above noted lapses.
observations.reason: Various deficiencies were still noted in the review of the procurement activities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor remind the BAC of its duties and responsibilities relative to purchases of goods through the different mode of procurement mentioned in R.A. No. 9184. Also, remind the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, to adhere to the basic documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Municipal Accountant and the BAC to complete the supporting documents.
observations.reason: Various deficiencies were still noted in the review of the procurement activities of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant, as the internal auditor, to see to it that all the necessary documentary requirement attached to the vouchers are accurate and complete before processing the claims.
observations.reason: Lacking documentary requirements in the payments on various infrastructure projects of the Municipality were still noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor along with all the concerned officials and employees should strictly identify and implement programs/ projects/projects/activities that are in line with social and economic development as well as environmental management concerns of the community in accordance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM.

We also advised the management that any realignment or changes made in the budget or projects indicated in the AIP should be supported by MDC/SB Resolutions to ensure the legality of the changes or realignment made.  These documents should be furnished to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: There are still disbursements for CY 2016 which do not partake the nature of investment or capital expenditure.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee furnish the Audit Team with the complete and accurate RPCI and RPCPPE duly reconciled with the property records and accounting records pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

We recommended that Management:
stop the practice of procuring goods through reimbursement and strictly comply with the guidelines that regular, recurring and non-petty expenses should be paid in check in the name of the supplier so that applicable taxes may be deducted thereon; and
adhere to the provisions of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 on the adoption of shopping as an alternative mode of procurement.

We recommended that Management:
strictly impose compliance with COA Circular No. 2013-004 and ensure timely submission of a complete list of all on-going projects/programs/activities at the start of CY 2016 and ensuing years containing the following:
project name
implementing unit, office or division if it is not the agency as a whole
brief description of the PPA
contractor or supplier, if any
mode of procurement
funding source
cost or approved budget
project duration including start and completion dates
location;
provide the Supervising Auditor and Audit Team Leader the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of infrastructure projects or before the start of the program/activity; and
post the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices for the SA and ATL to validate the same.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the general limitations on personal services cited under Section 325 (a) of R.A. No. 7160 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.  The Municipal Mayor, being the Local Chief Executive, should always be guided by the conditions of the agency’s annual and supplemental budgets to ensure that the budgeting requirements are completely complied with. As part of his fiscal responsibility, she should allow only payments of personal services costs and other expenditures expressly authorized by law.
observations.reason: Management still exceeded its 55% PS Limitation by P1,735,866.44 per budget and P1,747,697.80 per actual expenditure. Reiterated in Finding No. 2

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for all the LGU’s bank accounts to the Audit Team together with the paid checks, debit/credit memos for verification as provided for under Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 96-011. Also make an extra effort to submit the bank reconciliation statements on time.
observations.reason: The Management still incurred delay in  submission of Bank Reconcilaition Statements due to the late issuance of bank statements by the Agency’s depository banks.
